speaker: MATT
raw_text: Welcome back. Oh my god. Welcome back. Last we left off: The Mighty Nein, upon clearing their names, making some new friendships in the city and taking some time for themselves in preparation for the coming Harvest Close festival, found themselves wandering through the streets of celebration. They partook in a number of various games of chance and skill, got some fine snacks and foods, challenged themselves, got thrown by their other party members, won some trinkets and toys, and then ended up signing up to the Victory Pit, the grand tournament that ends the Harvest Close festival. You managed to catch a few phrases off to the side of the initial lounge you were signing up in, where you discovered that war had been declared by the king on the eastern region of Xhorhas, that information was to be held back until the festival was over. You were held below with the other competing teams, and then one by one you were each presented into the center of the Victory Pit tournament where each of you has to battle a challenge of a creature or creatures in a three-tiered structure. Whoever survives all three bouts gets to share in the final pot prize. Currently two of the other teams have been knocked out, and it only remains to be you and one other. You guys are up first. You're pulled into the center and announced as The Mighty Nein's final round. Knowing the Stubborn Stock is watching from behind the distant doorways that lead into the central arena, you all scattered about the sands as the sun begins to get close to dusk, shouting out your various plans and then abandoning them last minute. You found places as these heaping, heavy thuds start quaking the ground in arrival before the door swings open. Out from behind it, roaring, a frenzied, angry hill giant, complete with pierced thorns jammed into each side of the body to drive it to a point of absolute anger and fury, comes bashing into the center of the arena, knocks over one of the large pillars, and this is where we begin. I need everyone to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You face planted on the couch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've never seen Foster sprout tears like that. That was amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You know you've got him when he has projectile tears. It seems opposite of what should be happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A special shout out to our friend, PulloutKing, from that episode.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: PulloutKing, thanks for the laughs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We have new d20s restocked?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're not new, but older d20s

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're used d20s.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm pretty sure they're the special edition ones. I haven't looked at the store, they're the red set, I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The rare ones.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The ones that were available at our live shows before are in the store.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Laura, would you consider selling used dice that end up in the jail too often? Just offload some of that shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't think the store can handle that many.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think if the dice know that they could go to a kinder, gentler home.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think I may have enough dice to go to-- one for each and every critter.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's funny, you go and try to pet her dice they shrink back, they shy away. (squeaking)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're like barnacles!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Next charity drive we have you could auction a few.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Charity drive?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I was like  barnacles! She already said that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Guys, the birthday girl is trying to talk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry, never mind, it's passed. No, I was thinking you could auction them off for charity next time, next charity drive.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, yeah, charity. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I believe that's the last of our announcements of the evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. A fucking troll, you guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let's bring it in for tonight's episode of Critical Role. [click, TV static] [groovy Critical Role theme]
